subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Differential Equations
stimulus:
The rate at which a baby bird gains weight is proportional to the difference between its adult weight and its
current weight. At time t (cid:32) 0, when the bird is first weighed, its weight is 20 grams. If B(cid:11)t(cid:12) is the weight of the
bird, in grams, at time t days after it is first weighed, then
dB 1
(cid:32) (cid:11)100 (cid:16) B(cid:12).
dt 5
Let y (cid:32) B(cid:11)t(cid:12) be the solution to the differential equation above with initial condition B(cid:11)0(cid:12) (cid:32) 20.

parts.prompt:
Is the bird gaining weight faster when it weighs 40 grams or when it weighs 70 grams? Explain your
reasoning.
d2B d2B

scoring_points.description: Correct comparison: gaining weight faster at B = 40

parts.prompt:
Find in terms of B. Use to explain why the graph of B cannot resemble the following graph.
dt2 dt

scoring_points.description: Correct explanation: graph cannot have inflection (always concave down)

required_elements: Since $B < 100$, $d^2B/dt^2 < 0$ always — B is always concave down; no inflection point

common_traps: Stating the solution can be concave up

parts.prompt:
Use separation of variables to find y (cid:32) B(cid:11)t(cid:12), the particular solution to the differential equation with initial
condition B(cid:11)0(cid:12) (cid:32) 20.

common_traps: Failing to separate variables — earns 0 if variables not separated

scoring_points.description: Initial condition B(0) = 20 applied, solves for particular solution
